items.question_text: Under 5-8-1a, when vectoring a departing aircraft on a radar SID, concurrent use of a _____ is not permitted.
items.explanation: 5-8-1a: concurrent use of a diverse vector area (DVA) is not permitted when vectoring on a radar SID.

items.question_text: Under 5-8-2a, before departure, assign the initial heading consistent with either a SID being flown or _____, if applicable.
items.explanation: The initial heading must be consistent with a SID or a DVA (Diverse Vector Area) if applicable.

items.question_text: Under 5-8-5 TERMINAL, simultaneous operations are authorized between a departing aircraft and an arrival on final to another parallel or nonintersecting diverging runway, provided what two conditions are met?
items.explanation: 5-8-5 requires: (1) the runways are separated by at least 2,500 feet, or (2) a letter of agreement authorizes the operation for runways separated by less than 2,500 feet.

choices.text: Runways separated by at least 2,500 feet or authorized by LOA for less separation.
All departing aircraft must use the same runway.
The arrival must be at least 10 miles from the airport.
Visual separation must be provided by the tower.

items.situation: A departing aircraft is following a radar SID, and you also have a published DVA covering the same area. You want to vector the aircraft for separation while it is still on the SID.
items.question_text: Under 5-8-1, may you vector the aircraft using the DVA while it is on a radar SID?

choices.text: No, concurrent use of a DVA is not permitted when vectoring on a radar SID.
Yes, both the SID and DVA can be used together for maximum flexibility.
Yes, but only after the aircraft reaches the MVA.
Yes, if the pilot requests radar vectors.

items.explanation: 5-8-1A explicitly prohibits concurrent use of a DVA when vectoring a departing aircraft on a radar SID.
